question: Could you help me generate a report of CT image series from the dicom_all table such that all series from the NLST collection are excluded, any localizers or JPEG-compressed series (transfer syntaxes 1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.70 or 1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.51) are skipped, and only those passing certain geometry checks—namely a single orientation, identical pixel spacing, matching SOP instance and position counts, uniform pixel rows and columns, and a near-unity dot product of image orientation vectors—are included, while also computing slice interval differences, exposure differences, and approximate series size in MB for each qualified series?
evidence: Assumptions: exclude NLST collection; filter out JPEG compression formats: 1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.70 (JPEG Lossless, Non-Hierarchical, First-Order Prediction) and 1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.51 (JPEG Baseline (Process 1)); exclude localizer image type; all instances in a series must have identical values for image orientation (patient) and pixel spacing (converted to string); dot product between cross product of first two orientation vectors and [0,0,1] must be 1 ± 0.01; number of instances equals number of distinct Image Position (Patient) values (string comparison); identical first two components of Image Position (Patient); identical pixel rows and columns; compute difference between sorted third components of Image Position (Patient). Output report includes: series unique identifier, series number in study, study unique identifier, patient unique identifier, maximum dot product value, total SOP instances, distinct slice thickness values, max/min difference between consecutive slice intervals (z-coordinate spacing), tolerance for slice interval differences, distinct exposure values, max/min exposure value, exposure range (max-min), total series size in megabytes (MiB). Sort by slice interval difference tolerance, maximum exposure difference, series unique ID (all descending).
